text: Hello everyone, so let's solve this problem. The displacement of a particle starting from rest at t equal to 0 is given by s is equal to 60 square minus t cube. The time in which the particle will obtain 0 velocity again. So we have to find the time at which the particle has the velocity equal to 0. So first we will find the function of the velocity that is velocity is given by derivative of the displacement function that is ds by dt. So from here the derivative of this function, this 60 square minus t cube that will be equal to 6 multiplied by the derivative of t square that will be 2t minus the derivative of t cube will be 3t square. So this is our velocity function that is a 12t minus 3t square and now we have to just put it equal to 0 according to the condition in the question. So after taking 3 common and t common we get 3t and here this we get s 4 minus t and this is equal to 0. So one value of time is t equal to 0 which was the initial condition of the question that starting from the rest and another value of time can be given by this factor. So we get this s t minus 4 equal to 0 or we can say t equal to 4. So 4 second is the time at which the particle again comes to rest. So answer for this question which will be option number b. Thank you. I hope you understand this solution.
